title.value: Stop Overthinking: A Survey on Efficient Reasoning for Large Language Models
title.evidence: Stop Overthinking: A Survey on Efficient Reasoning for Large Language Models

background.value: Large Language Models (LLMs) have emerged as exceptionally powerful AI tools, demonstrating advanced capabilities in natural language understanding and complex reasoning.
background.evidence: Large Language Models (LLMs) have emerged as exceptionally powerful AI tools, demonstrating advanced capabilities in natural language understanding and complex reasoning.

problem_statement.value: The 'overthinking phenomenon' in long CoT reasoning models refers to situations where LLMs generate excessively detailed or unnecessarily elaborate reasoning steps, ultimately reducing their problem-solving efficiency.
problem_statement.evidence: The 'overthinking phenomenon' in long CoT reasoning models refers to situations where LLMs generate excessively detailed or unnecessarily elaborate reasoning steps, ultimately reducing their problem-solving efficiency.

provenance.section_header: 2.3 The Overthinking Problem in Long CoT Reasoning Models

description.value: Model-based efficient reasoning focuses on fine-tuning LLMs to improve their intrinsic ability to reason concisely and efficiently.
description.evidence: Model-based efficient reasoning focuses on fine-tuning LLMs to improve their intrinsic ability to reason concisely and efficiently.

examples.value: DeepSeek-R1 and OpenAI o1 utilize reinforcement learning with length reward design to optimize reasoning length.
examples.evidence: DeepSeek-R1 and OpenAI o1 utilize reinforcement learning with length reward design to optimize reasoning length.

description.value: Output-based efficient reasoning modifies the output paradigm to enhance the ability of LLMs to reason concisely and efficiently.
description.evidence: Output-based efficient reasoning modifies the output paradigm to enhance the ability of LLMs to reason concisely and efficiently.

examples.value: Methods like Coconut and CODI leverage latent representations to compress reasoning steps.
examples.evidence: Methods like Coconut and CODI leverage latent representations to compress reasoning steps.

provenance.section_header: 4.1 Compressing Reasoning Steps into Fewer Latent Representation

description.value: Prompt-guided efficient reasoning explicitly instructs LLMs to generate fewer reasoning steps.
description.evidence: Prompt-guided efficient reasoning explicitly instructs LLMs to generate fewer reasoning steps.

examples.value: Techniques like Token-Budget and Concise Chain-of-Thought (CCoT) prompting guide LLMs to perform concise reasoning.
examples.evidence: Techniques like Token-Budget and Concise Chain-of-Thought (CCoT) prompting guide LLMs to perform concise reasoning.

performance_metrics.value: The paper evaluates the reasoning capabilities of LLMs using benchmarks like Sys2Bench and S1-Bench.
performance_metrics.evidence: The paper evaluates the reasoning capabilities of LLMs using benchmarks like Sys2Bench and S1-Bench.

results_summary.value: Efficient reasoning approaches significantly enhance performance across various domains, including healthcare and autonomous driving.
results_summary.evidence: Efficient reasoning approaches significantly enhance performance across various domains, including healthcare and autonomous driving.

conclusions.value: This survey categorizes efficient reasoning approaches and highlights their practical benefits in various applications.
conclusions.evidence: This survey categorizes efficient reasoning approaches and highlights their practical benefits in various applications.
